title: herniaria boissieri subsp. boissieri
intro: Herniaria boissieri subsp. boissieri is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It is also known as Boissier's rampion or lesser rampion, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as aiding in digestion, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herniaria boissieri subsp. boissieri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
